channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Tackling the Future of Wheat Production in a Changing Climate
item.description: Wheat, a staple crop for billions, is facing significant challenges due to climate change. Although the plant can withstand droughts, heat, and cold, the increasing severity of weather events and shif...

item.title: Toronto Subway Buskers Spread Joy During Rush Hour
item.description: In the hustle and bustle of Toronto's subway system, musicians like Leo Zhang and Benji Crane are brightening commuters' days through their music. These buskers, part of a long-standing tradition in t...

item.title: Kiambu Land Dispute: Paradise Lost Co-Owner Alleges Exclusion by Brothers Amid Alleged Land Grabbing
item.description: In Kiambu, Daniel Fundi, the eldest of the Mbugua brothers and co-owner of the Paradise Lost leisure facility, has raised serious allegations against his siblings, claiming he has been sidelined from ...

item.title: Tanzania Commission Confirms 518 Deaths in 2025 Post-Election Violence
item.description: Tanzania's Commission of Inquiry, led by retired Chief Justice Mohamed Chande Othman, has revealed findings regarding the post-election violence that erupted in 2025. The report, released on April 23,...

item.title: Calls for Privileges Committee Review After Starmer Sacks Sir Olly Robbins Amid Mandelson Vetting Controversy
item.description: Former Labour MP Karl Turner has criticised Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer for sacking Sir Olly Robbins, describing it as the 'biggest act of political self-harm' he has witnessed. Turner, who recent...

item.title: US-Iran Ceasefire Extended Amid Diverging Interpretations
item.description: The ceasefire between the United States and Iran, initially announced for two weeks, has been extended but remains fragile and poorly defined. While both nations agreed to halt direct military strikes...

item.title: Jamie Sarkonak reflects on 'The Camp of the Saints' and its relevance to debates on migration and censorship
item.description: In this opinion piece, Jamie Sarkonak discusses Jean Raspail’s controversial 1973 dystopian novel The Camp of the Saints, arguing that its themes remain relevant to contemporary debates on migration a...

item.title: Bala Mohammed and Peter Obi discuss opposition cooperation ahead of 2027 elections
item.description: Bauchi State Governor, Bala Mohammed, don describe himself as a “freelance politician” after him meet former presidential candidate Peter Obi for closed-door talks in Bauchi. The meeting happen agains...

item.title: Senate Democrats Talarico and Ossoff report record‑level fundraising in key races
item.description: Senate Democratic candidates are reporting exceptionally strong fundraising totals for the first quarter of 2026, signaling robust small‑dollar support ahead of high‑stakes battles for control of the ...

item.title: Iran detains vessels in Strait of Hormuz as US pauses strikes and tensions remain high
item.description: Iran has seized two ships in the Strait of Hormuz, increasing tensions in a region that is already very sensitive for global energy supply. According to Iranian sources, the vessels were taken over by...

item.title: Tamil Nadu and West Bengal Witness Historic Voter Turnout in Elections
item.description: In a historic electoral moment, Tamil Nadu and West Bengal have recorded their highest-ever voter turnout since India gained Independence. According to the Election Commission (EC), both states saw re...

item.title: Ramaphosa places SAPS commissioner Masemola on precautionary suspension, appoints Dimpane as acting head
item.description: President Cyril Ramaphosa has placed national police commissioner Fannie Masemola on precautionary suspension with immediate effect, pending the outcome of formal criminal proceedings. This makes Mase...

item.title: Rising ocean temperatures threaten warm-bodied predatory fish

item.title: Why workplace abuse continues despite policies and frameworks
item.description: Despite organizations implementing policies to address sexual exploitation, abuse, and harassment in the workplace, experts argue that the root issue lies in the failure to properly address these inci...

item.title: First-time voters in Chennai and Thoothukudi share their excitement at casting votes
item.description: In Chennai and Thoothukudi, first-time voters were filled with excitement and nervousness as they cast their votes for the first time. Vishwaa J., an 18-year-old college student from Chennai, expresse...

item.title: Understanding Tanzania National Debt Sustainability Beyond the Gross Figure
item.description: The Controller and Auditor General recently highlighted that Tanzania's total national debt reached 52.1 billion US dollars as of February 2026. While some commentators and MPs view this as alarming, ...

item.title: Fire at Montreal Recycling Plant Prompts Health Warning and Permit Revocation
item.description: A fire broke out early Thursday morning at the American Iron and Metal (AIM) recycling facility in Montréal-Est, sending a thick plume of white smoke over Montreal. The fire, originating in the plant'...

item.title: Tanzania Cashew Processors Urge Government to Improve Sector Support and Access to Technology
item.description: Cashew processors in Tanzania are urging the government to make the business environment better, strengthen value addition, and expand local processing. The sector faces ongoing challenges like outdat...

item.title: Nigeria Records Highest-Ever ICAO Aviation Safety Score at 91.45%, Tinubu Reacts
item.description: President Bola Tinubu don welcome Nigeria’s latest aviation safety rating of 91.45 per cent from the International Civil Aviation Organisation (ICAO), describing am as a big achievement for the countr...

item.title: Tanzanian commission recommends reconciliation, justice, and constitutional reforms after 2025 election unrest
item.description: A Tanzanian commission investigating unrest after the October 2025 general election has urged comprehensive measures to restore national unity and strengthen governance. Led by Judge Chande Othman, th...
